rationale: The citing paper gives Gielens (2012) a specific functional role in its empirical design, not just a background mention. In the variable operationalization section, the authors state that their brand-leadership coding approach is based on Gielens (2012), using that study to justify treating the top-share brand as uniquely positioned. This citation therefore supports construction and interpretation of a key moderator in their model.
relation_description: The citing study applies Gielens (2012) to operationalize brand leadership, coding the highest market-share brand as the category leader and using prior evidence on leader uniqueness to justify this choice.
